27.kj: [idiom] as, but, for(-asmuch [phrase]), [phrase] now, of, seeing, than, that, therefore, until, [phrase] what (-soever), when, which, whom, whose.

27.gl: {properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every (in the singular only, but often in a plural sense)}
27.kj: all, any, + (forasmuch) as, + be-(for this) cause, every, + no (manner, -ne), + there (where) -fore, + though, what (where, who) -soever, (the) whole.

27.gl: {the sky (as aloft; the dual perhaps alluding to the visible arch in which the clouds move, as well as to the higher ether where the celestial bodies revolve)}
{a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of Israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock}
{to give (whether literal or figurative); generally, to put; imperatively (reflexive) come}
27.kj: deliver, give, lay, [phrase] prolong, pay, yield.

27.gl: {to hear intelligently (often with implication of attention, obedience, etc.; causatively, to tell, etc.)}

28.gl: that, used as relative conjunction, and especially (with a preposition) in adverbial phrases; also as preposition of
28.kj: [idiom] as, but, for(-asmuch [phrase]), [phrase] now, of, seeing, than, that, therefore, until, [phrase] what (-soever), when, which, whom, whose.

28.gl: {above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications}
28.kj: about, against, concerning, for, (there-) fore, from, in, [idiom] more, of, (there-, up-) on, (in-) to, [phrase] why with.

28.gl: {as far (or long, or much) as, whether of space (even unto) or time (during, while, until) or degree (equally with)}
28.kj: [idiom] and, at, for, (hither-) to, on till, (un-) to, until, within.

28.gl: {the heart; also used (figuratively) very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything}
{properly, like this, i.e. by implication, (of manner) thus (or so); also (of place) here (or hither); or (of time) now}
large (in size, quantity or number, also adverbial)
28.kj: exceeding, great(-ly); many, much, sore, very.
